title: Datashading: millions of points, honestly
text: datashade() bins xlim ylim returns raster fills viewport (npc 0..1). place data axes, draw inside vl_viewport() whose xscale / yscale match limits, draw axes, labels, reference lines "native" units viewport. crisp bins, match width / height viewport’s pixel size keep default interpolate = FALSE. attractors centre square window orbit square cell maps without distortion: Passing window xlim / ylim keeps aspect honest regardless raw orbit range differs two axes.
code: window <- function(x, y, pad = 1.05) {   xr <- range(x)   yr <- range(y)   half <- max(diff(xr), diff(yr)) / 2 * pad   list(     xlim = mean(xr) + c(-half, half),     ylim = mean(yr) + c(-half, half)   ) }  w <- window(pts$x, pts$y) str(w) #> List of 2 #>  $ xlim: num [1:2] -1.5 2.06 #>  $ ylim: num [1:2] -1.67 1.9

title: Datashading: millions of points, honestly
text: shaded attractor just grob, gallery layout . Push grid_layout() "null" (flexible) tracks drop one datashade() raster cell. Square cells (square window ) keep every map undistorted.  shipped example (inst/examples/attractors.R) goes : draws twelve panels random parameters, keeping orbits fill fair fraction canvas (rejection sampling, since random parameters collapse point diverge), random colormap. Run fresh gallery every time:
code: gallery <- list(   list(kind = "clifford", p = c(-1.4,  1.6,  1.0,  0.7),        pal = c("#ffffff", "#c6dbef", "#6baed6", "#2171b5", "#08306b")),   list(kind = "dejong",   p = c( 1.4, -2.3,  2.4, -2.1),        pal = c("#ffffff", "#fdd0a2", "#fd8d3c", "#d94801", "#7f2704")),   list(kind = "svensson", p = c( 1.5, -1.8,  1.6,  0.9),        pal = c("#ffffff", "#c7e9c0", "#74c476", "#238b45", "#00441b")),   list(kind = "clifford", p = c(-1.8, -2.0, -0.5, -0.9),        pal = c("#ffffff", "#dadaeb", "#9e9ac8", "#6a51a3", "#3f007d")) )  ncol <- 2 nrow <- 2 cell_px <- 300  s <- vl_scene(6, 6, dpi = 100, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(layout = grid_layout(     widths  = vl_unit(rep(1, ncol), "null"),     heights = vl_unit(rep(1, nrow), "null")   )))  for (i in seq_along(gallery)) {   a <- gallery[[i]]   orbit <- attractor(a$kind, a$p)   w <- window(orbit$x, orbit$y)   img <- datashade(     orbit$x, orbit$y,     width = cell_px, height = cell_px,     xlim = w$xlim, ylim = w$ylim,     colors = a$pal, how = "eq_hist"   )   row <- (i - 1) %/% ncol + 1   col <- (i - 1) %% ncol + 1   s <- s |> push(vl_viewport(row = row, col = col)) |> draw(img) |> pop() }  s Rscript inst/examples/attractors.R gallery.png 1e7

title: Datashading: millions of points, honestly
text: Points thing overplots. hundred stacked timeseries, thousand samples, already million line vertices — draw vector paths get solid band hides traces actually concentrate. aggregate--shade idea applies: rasterise lines grid cell accumulates coverage lines crossing , colour density. datashade_lines() . takes connected polyline. Pass group pack many series one call — line breaks wherever group id changes (NA coordinate breaks ), whole panel traces single call single raster.  bright core walks pile (near start, diffuse apart); faint outer envelope handful traces wandered far. structure exactly opaque band 480,000 vertices hidden. Coverage anti-aliased summed, overlapping traces brighten honestly rather saturating single flat colour.
code: set.seed(1) k <- 600       # series m <- 800       # samples each steps <- matrix(rnorm(k * m, sd = 0.4), m, k) walks <- apply(steps, 2, cumsum)          # k random walks, m samples  t <- rep(seq_len(m), times = k)           # x: shared time axis y <- as.vector(walks)                     # y: all traces concatenated grp <- rep(seq_len(k), each = m)          # which series each vertex belongs to  vl_scene(6, 4, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(xscale = range(t), yscale = range(y))) |>   draw(datashade_lines(     t, y, group = grp,     width = 600, height = 400,     colors = c("#ffffff", "#fdd0a2", "#fd8d3c", "#d94801", "#7f2704"),     how = "eq_hist"   ))

title: Datashading: millions of points, honestly
text: datashade_segments() engine independent segments — element one (x0, y0) -> (x1, y1) line, connected path. motivating case edges large graph: hairball tens thousands edges drawn vectors unreadable blob, aggregated becomes edge-density field showing regions layout edges actually run .  Per-segment weight works like point path’s per-point weight: pass edge weights cells accumulate summed weight instead plain crossing count.
code: set.seed(2) # A toy "layout": two loose clusters of nodes with many edges between them. n_nodes <- 400 nx <- c(rnorm(n_nodes / 2, -1), rnorm(n_nodes / 2, 1)) ny <- rnorm(n_nodes) n_edges <- 8000 a <- sample(n_nodes, n_edges, replace = TRUE) b <- sample(n_nodes, n_edges, replace = TRUE)  vl_scene(6, 4, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(xscale = range(nx), yscale = range(ny))) |>   draw(datashade_segments(     nx[a], ny[a], nx[b], ny[b],     width = 600, height = 400,     colors = c("#ffffff", "#c6dbef", "#6baed6", "#2171b5", "#08306b"),     how = "eq_hist"   ))

title: Rendering grid and ggplot2 through vellum
text: grid graphics lazy: grob’s real geometry known device viewport resolve units. as_vellum() leans machinery rather reimplementing . spins offscreen grid device, lets grid resolve every coordinate absolute position, emits corresponding vellum grobs. output normal vellum_scene, everything else vellum (multi-backend render(), scene_raster(), display()) applies.

title: Rendering grid and ggplot2 through vellum
text: grob gTree works. small hand-built one.  as_vellum() returns scene, auto-prints (displays) . Assign can render backend:
code: library(grid)  g <- gTree(children = gList(   rectGrob(gp = grid::gpar(fill = "grey97", col = "grey70")),   circleGrob(r = 0.3, gp = grid::gpar(fill = "#3a7bd5", col = NA)),   textGrob("grid -> vellum", y = 0.12, gp = grid::gpar(fontface = "bold")) ))  as_vellum(g, width = 5, height = 3) render_grid(g, "grid.png", width = 5, height = 3) render_grid(g, "grid.pdf", width = 5, height = 3)

what: When to use interop versus the native API

Build native vl_scene() API instead want retained scene graph offers: named editable nodes, hit-testing, per-element scene_model() (see vignette("retained-mode")). Interop reproduces pixels grid scene, grammar built directly vellum carries per-element identity interactivity needs. native vellum graphics device (plot() friends target vellum directly) future work; , as_vellum() bridge. ```

title: Retained-mode superpowers: hit-testing and editable nodes
text: Every grob viewport takes optional name. name turns node something can look modify later. node_names() lists names paint order, get_node() returns node , can inspect value built earlier.
code: dots <- vl_scene(5, 3, bg = "white") |>   draw(circle_grob(x = 0.25, y = 0.5, r = 0.16, name = "a",                    gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#bdc3c7", col = NA))) |>   draw(circle_grob(x = 0.55, y = 0.5, r = 0.16, name = "b",                    gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#bdc3c7", col = NA))) |>   draw(circle_grob(x = 0.85, y = 0.5, r = 0.16, name = "c",                    gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#bdc3c7", col = NA)))  node_names(dots) #> [1] "a" "b" "c" get_node(dots, "b") #> <vellum::grob_circle> #>  @ name  : chr "b" #>  @ gp    : <vellum::vl_gpar> #>  .. @ col       : logi NA #>  .. @ fill      : chr "#bdc3c7" #>  .. @ lwd       : NULL #>  .. @ alpha     : NULL #>  .. @ lty       : NULL #>  .. @ lineend   : NULL #>  .. @ linejoin  : NULL #>  .. @ linemitre : NULL #>  .. @ fontfamily: NULL #>  .. @ fontface  : NULL #>  .. @ fontsize  : NULL #>  .. @ lineheight: NULL #>  @ vp    : NULL #>  @ id    : NULL #>  @ role  : NULL #>  @ keys  : NULL #>  @ meta  : NULL #>  @ x     : unit [1:1] 0.55npc #>  @ y     : unit [1:1] 0.5npc #>  @ r     : unit [1:1] 0.16npc #>  @ sketch: NULL

title: The scene graph and the paint model
text: vellum scene tree. root page created vl_scene(); every push() adds vl_viewport() child descends ; every draw() appends grob current level; pop() climbs back . tree retained rather drawn--forgotten, enables queries vignette("retained-mode"). tree, viewports nest, child’s geometry expressed relative parent. whole mechanism behind panels, insets, faceting: push viewport sub-region, draw inside local coordinates, pop.
code: vl_scene(6, 2.4, bg = "white") |>   # a full-width band   draw(rect_grob(height = 0.6, gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#eef2f6", col = NA))) |>   # an inset viewport occupying the middle third   push(vl_viewport(x = 0.5, width = 1 / 3, height = 0.8)) |>   draw(rect_grob(gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#3a7bd5", col = NA))) |>   draw(text_grob("inset", gp = vl_gpar(col = "white", fontface = "bold"))) |>   pop()

title: The scene graph and the paint model
text: Coordinates sizes vl_unit() vectors: value paired unit name. element carries unit, one vector can mix coordinate systems, grob can even use different units x y axes. units reach : "npc" (default): normalised parent coordinates, 0 bottom/left 1 top/right current viewport. "native": enclosing viewport’s xscale / yscale, data values map directly. use plotted data. "mm", "cm", "", "pt": absolute physical lengths keep size regardless viewport. bare number interpreted grob’s default units (usually "npc"), x = 0.5 x = vl_unit(0.5, "npc") thing. "native" units need viewport scales resolve . Set xscale yscale push:  Absolute relative units compose within viewport, font- string-relative units ("char", "line", "strwidth") resolve millimetres construction. Mixing relative absolute unit single arithmetic expression (say vl_unit(1, "npc") - vl_unit(2, "mm")) deferred reported rather silently guessed, ambiguous offset fails loudly instead drawing wrong place.
code: vl_unit(1:3, "native") #> <vellum_unit[3]> #> [1] 1native 2native 3native vl_unit(c(0.5, 1), c("npc", "in")) #> <vellum_unit[2]> #> [1] 0.5npc 1.0in vl_scene(5, 3, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(     width = 0.86, height = 0.82,     xscale = c(0, 10), yscale = c(-5, 25)   )) |>   draw(rect_grob(gp = vl_gpar(fill = "grey97", col = "grey70"))) |>   draw(lines_grob(     x = vl_unit(0:10, "native"),     y = vl_unit((0:10) * 2, "native"),     gp = vl_gpar(col = "steelblue", lwd = 2)   )) |>   pop()

title: The scene graph and the paint model
text: linear_gradient() radial_gradient() interpolate colour stops. geometry given coordinate system ("npc" default) resolved viewport draw time, gradient transforms grob just like outline .  Stops blend sRGB default. Blending two distant hues passes muddy, -dark middle — blue→yellow ramp dips grey. Set interpolation = "oklab" blend perceptually-uniform Oklab space instead, ramp stays even vivid. option works every backend (stops pre-sampled Oklab ordinary sRGB stops), default "srgb" unchanged.
code: vl_scene(6, 2.2, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(x = 0.28, width = 0.44)) |>   draw(rect_grob(     width = 0.8, height = 0.7,     gp = vl_gpar(fill = linear_gradient(c("#1b2a4a", "#3a7bd5")), col = NA)   )) |>   pop() |>   push(vl_viewport(x = 0.72, width = 0.44)) |>   draw(circle_grob(     r = 0.34,     gp = vl_gpar(fill = radial_gradient(c("#f6d365", "#fda085")), col = NA)   )) |>   pop() bar <- function(x, interp) {   rect_grob(     x = x, width = 0.46, height = 0.7,     gp = vl_gpar(col = NA, fill = linear_gradient(c("blue", "yellow"), interpolation = interp))   ) } vl_scene(6, 1.6, bg = "white") |>   draw(bar(0.27, "srgb")) |> # sRGB: grey dead-zone in the middle   draw(bar(0.73, "oklab")) # Oklab: clean cyan/green transition

title: Get started
text: scene starts vl_scene(), fixes page size (inches default) background. add content pipeline draw() calls show result. Auto-printing scene displays , last line chunk enough.  Three ideas work . Grobs drawable primitives: rect_grob(), circle_grob(), text_grob(), couple dozen (see ?grob). immutable value object; building one draws nothing . vl_gpar() carries graphical parameters (fill, stroke colour, line width, font, opacity) attached grob. fill can plain colour gradient, . Coordinates default "npc": normalised parent coordinates, (0, 0) bottom-left region (1, 1) top-right. x = 0.16 sits near left edge y = 0.5 vertically centred.
code: vl_scene(width = 6, height = 3, bg = "white") |>   draw(rect_grob(     width = 0.94, height = 0.82,     gp = vl_gpar(fill = linear_gradient(c("#1b2a4a", "#3a7bd5")), col = NA)   )) |>   draw(circle_grob(     x = 0.16, y = 0.5, r = 0.28,     gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#f7c948", col = NA)   )) |>   draw(text_grob(     "vellum", x = 0.62, y = 0.5,     gp = vl_gpar(fontsize = 48, col = "white", fontface = "bold")   ))

what: Building a scene: push, draw, pop
title: Get started
text: scene tree viewports grobs, built functionally. push() descends new vl_viewport(), draw() adds grob current level, pop() ascends. viewport rectangular region establishes coordinate systems, push one xscale yscale, "native" units map data values straight onto region.  panel rectangle drawn "npc" (fills viewport), points placed "native" units, positions follow data scales. single vl_unit() vector can even mix coordinate systems per axis. See vignette("scene--paint") full picture units, viewports, paint model.
code: set.seed(1) x <- runif(60, 0, 10) y <- 1.8 * x + rnorm(60, 0, 4)  vl_scene(5, 3.2, bg = "white") |>   push(vl_viewport(     x = 0.57, y = 0.57, width = 0.82, height = 0.82,     xscale = c(0, 10), yscale = range(pretty(y))   )) |>   draw(rect_grob(gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#f4f6f8", col = "#cfd8dc"))) |>   draw(points_grob(     vl_unit(x, "native"), vl_unit(y, "native"),     size = vl_unit(3.2, "mm"),     gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#3a7bd5", col = "#1b2a4a", lwd = 1)   )) |>   pop()

code: library(vellum)  vl_scene(width = 5, height = 2.4, bg = "white") |>   draw(rect_grob(width = 0.94, height = 0.82,                  gp = vl_gpar(fill = linear_gradient(c("#1b2a4a", "#3a7bd5")), col = NA))) |>   draw(circle_grob(x = 0.16, y = 0.5, r = 0.28,                    gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#f7c948", col = NA))) |>   draw(text_grob("vellum", x = 0.62, y = 0.5,                  gp = vl_gpar(fontsize = 64, col = "white", fontface = "bold"))) |>   render("man/figures/README-hello.png")

title: A low-level graphics framework with a Rust backend
text: Rust backend, deterministic output. Rendering runs Rust (tiny-skia raster, krilla PDF) byte-stable, output reproducible snapshot-testable. One scene, three backends. scene renders PNG, SVG, PDF consistent geometry, via render(scene, "plot.svg") render(scene, "plot.pdf"). retained scene graph, immediate-mode drawing. scene kept (drawn--forgotten), vellum offers capabilities grid : hit-testing (hit_test() picks topmost grob point) named, editable nodes (node_names() / get_node() / edit_node()). modern paint model across backends. Linear & radial gradients, tiling patterns, alpha/luminance masks, group opacity (vl_viewport(alpha=)). Built-big-data aggregation. datashade() bins millions points density raster one pass, overplotting giant files. Device-independent, faithful text. Shaping runs textshaping (sits systemfonts) — stack ragg/svglite — per-glyph fallback, justification, rotation, Markdown-style rich labels (md(): bold/italic, super/subscript, coloured spans). Vectorised primitives flex layout engine. Batched rects/circles/points/segments/text, nested viewports rotation arbitrary-path clipping, row/column layout solver "null" (flexible) tracks. Grid interop. as_vellum() / render_grid() render existing grid grob tree, including ggplot2 lattice, vellum backend.

code: tile <- list(rect_grob(gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#ecf0f1", col = NA)),              circle_grob(r = 0.32, gp = vl_gpar(fill = "#e74c3c", col = NA)))  vl_scene(6, 2.1, bg = "white") |>   # radial gradient   push(vl_viewport(x = 1/6, width = 1/3)) |>     draw(circle_grob(r = 0.42, gp = vl_gpar(fill = radial_gradient(c("#f6d365", "#fda085")), col = NA))) |>   pop() |>   # tiling pattern   push(vl_viewport(x = 3/6, width = 1/3)) |>     draw(rect_grob(width = 0.84, height = 0.84,                    gp = vl_gpar(fill = vl_pattern(tile, width = 0.22, height = 0.22), col = NA))) |>   pop() |>   # a linear gradient, clipped to a circular mask   push(vl_viewport(x = 5/6, width = 1/3,                 mask = as_mask(circle_grob(r = 0.42, gp = vl_gpar(fill = "white", col = NA))))) |>     draw(rect_grob(gp = vl_gpar(fill = linear_gradient(c("#7f53ac", "#647dee")), col = NA))) |>   pop() |>   render("man/figures/README-paint.png")

title: Aggregate-then-shade a large point cloud (datashader-style) — datashade
text: x, y Point coordinates (plain numerics, data space). weight Optional per-point weight; cells accumulate summed weight instead plain count. NULL counts. scalar recycled every point; otherwise weight must length x. width, height Aggregation grid size cells (= output raster pixels). xlim, ylim Data range bin ; default finite range x/y. category Optional per-point category (factor vector) selecting categorical (count_cat) shading; see Details. NULL (default) shades plain density. colors density shading, two colours forming low--high ramp. categorical shading (category set), per-category hue vector — named category level, one colour per level level order. Density--colour mapping: "eq_hist" (histogram equalisation — datashader's default, reveals structure across orders magnitude), "log", "cbrt" (cube root), "linear". Also drives per-cell opacity categorical shading. span Optional c(lo, hi) density values mapped ends colour ramp / opacity range; densities outside clamped. NULL (default) uses full observed range. clip Optional percentile pair [0, 1] (e.g. c(0.01, 0.99)) deriving span quantiles non-empty cell densities — robust way keep extreme cells flattening rest. Overrides span. spread Optional post-aggregation spreading, applied shaded raster keep sparse output visible (see spread() / dynspread()): NULL (default) none; positive integer applies spread() pixel radius; "auto" applies dynspread() (radius chosen image density). interpolate Passed raster_grob(); FALSE keeps hard bin edges. name, vp, id, role Passed raster_grob() (see grob).

title: Gradient fills — gradients
text: colours vector two colours (R colour spec). stops = NULL spread evenly across [0, 1]. stops Optional offsets [0, 1], one per colour. Defaults evenly spaced. x1, y1, x2, y2 Start end points linear gradient (default left--right sweep npc). units Coordinate system geometry: one "npc", "native", "mm", "", "pt". extend gradient behaves outside [0, 1]: "pad" (clamp end stops), "repeat", "reflect". interpolation Colour space stops blended : "srgb" (default), "oklab" (perceptually uniform), "oklch" (perceptual, hue-preserving). See Details. cx, cy, r Centre radius radial gradient's outer circle — end ramp (stop offset 1). Default centred, radius 0.5 npc. fx, fy, fr Centre radius focal (start) circle — origin ramp (stop offset 0). Defaults (fx = cx, fy = cy, fr = 0) give ordinary concentric gradient; move fx/fy place highlight -centre, raise fr annular ramp two circles. Radii must non-negative.

title: Gradient fills — gradients
text: vellum_gradient object, suitable vl_gpar(fill = ...).

title: Gradient fills — gradients
text: radial gradient runs two circles: focal (start) circle fx/fy/fr stop offset 0 outer (end) circle cx/cy/r offset 1. default concentric (fx = cx, fy = cy, fr = 0) — classic centred highlight. Offsetting fx/fy moves highlight -centre (sphere lit one side); non-zero fr gives annular ramp two circles. default stops blended sRGB (backend's native behaviour). Set interpolation = "oklab" blend perceptually-uniform Oklab space instead, removes muddy, -dark midtones hue drift sRGB blending — ramp stays even vivid. interpolation = "oklch" blends polar form space (lightness, chroma, hue): hue chroma move independently, ramp two saturated colours keeps chroma middle instead dipping toward grey way straight line Oklab can — cost sweeping intermediate hues along shorter arc (e.g. blue→yellow passes green). modes work identically raster, SVG, PDF backends.

title: Graphical objects (grobs) — grob
text: x, y Coordinates (vl_unit() numeric). width, height Grob size (vl_unit() numeric), recycled like x/y. grobs drawn rectangle size. hexagon_grob(), optional per-hexagon full corner--corner extent along x/y: given override size (resolved per-axis, hexagon can non-regular tile non-square lattice — e.g. "native" units tile data space regardless device aspect); regular flat hexagon height == width * sqrt(3) / 2; leave NULL use circumradius size; must given together. loop_grob(), width instead dimensionless lateral petal scale (0, 1] (recycled per loop): 1 (default) full teardrop, smaller narrows petal's waist without shortening (igraph "narrowing" factor). sketch Optional sketch() spec hand-drawn look; NULL = crisp. gp Graphical parameters, vl_gpar(). name Optional name (edit_node()). vp Optional vl_viewport() draw grob inside. id grobs, optional semantic identifier emitted SVG backend data-vellum-id (interactivity, accessibility, testing; ignored raster/PDF). path_grob , id instead groups points (one value per point) closed sub-paths: points sharing id form one sub-path (hole separate id), first-appearance order (à la grid); NULL makes single sub-path. role Optional ARIA role, emitted SVG backend role= accessibility (ignored raster PDF backends). key Optional per-element data key(s) batched marks (points_grob, circle_grob, rect_grob, segments_grob, hexagon_grob, sector_grob), recycled element count like fill. Emitted SVG backend data-key element surfaced scene_model() — join key host uses map interaction back datum. NULL (default) emits nothing (static render unchanged). Ignored raster/PDF. meta Optional free-form per-element metadata batched marks: list one entry (record) per element (recycled), e.g. tooltip text field values. never reaches backend (nothing drawn changes); rides scene returned scene_model(). NULL (default) = none. r Radius (vl_unit() numeric). arrow vl_arrow() spec draw heads line/segment ends, NULL none. start_cap, end_cap Optional absolute-length vl_unit()s (mm/cm/ /pt; bare numeric taken mm) shorten drawn line inward start/end physical amount, resolved render device space — gap exact size, dpi, aspect ratio, reliance native scale. segments_grob() caps per-element (scalar length-n, recycled like coordinates); lines_grob() single (scalar) cap trims end whole polyline. NULL (default) leaves endpoint untouched. vl_arrow() also present head placed capped end, tip lands boundary (e.g. node marker) rather . lets directed edge stop node's radius. See acceptance notes package degenerate cases (cap >= segment length draws nothing; zero-length segment skipped). offset Optional absolute-length vl_unit() (mm/cm//pt; bare numeric mm) shifts line perpendicular direction physical amount, resolved render device space. sign picks side (+ left direction travel, − right). segments_grob() per-element (scalar length-n) — passing vector spreads parallel/reciprocal edges fixed physical spacing tracks mm node sizes figure size; lines_grob() single (scalar) offset rigidly translates whole polyline along perpendicular overall direction. Applied start_cap/end_cap arrowhead (offset, cap, head). NULL/0 (default) leaves geometry untouched. n Number points sample curve (flattened polyline). shape Marker shape(s): "circle" (default), "square", "triangle", "diamond", "plus", "cross", "triangle_down", "star", recycled per point. Filled shapes ("plus"/"cross") paint gp$fill outline gp$col, open marker fill = NA col, filled one sets fill; "plus"/"cross" stroke-line glyphs. open FALSE, spline closed (wraps end start). size Loop extent: absolute vl_unit() (mm/cm//pt; bare numeric mm), resolved device length render loop tracks node's mm size page size/dpi. Nested loops one vertex pass growing size (x/y/angle) concentric teardrops. Recycled per loop. fill Per-element fill colour(s), recycled number sectors. NULL falls back gp$fill. orientation Hexagon orientation: "flat" (default, flat top/bottom edge) "pointy" (vertex top). size circumradius (centre vertex). r0, r1 Inner outer radius sector (vl_unit() numeric; numeric treated "native"). r0 = 0 gives pie slice; r0 == r1 gives arc outline (stroke , fill). theta0, theta1 Start end angle sector, radians, 0 3 o'clock increasing counter-clockwise. foot Node radius loop's two feet attach (absolute vl_unit(); 0 = feet vertex, like igraph). positive foot puts feet node's boundary loop visibly leaves re-enters node edge, directed loop's head lands boundary rather marker. Recycled per loop. angle Outward direction loop radians (way teardrop bulges away vertex, e.g. away layout centroid). x0, y0, x1, y1 Segment start/end coordinates (vl_unit() numeric). rule Fill rule: "winding" (non-zero, default) "evenodd". image raster image: grDevices::.raster()-compatible object — matrix/array colours greyscale values, raster object. interpolate Smoothly interpolate scaling (default TRUE)? FALSE keeps hard pixel edges. label Character string(s) draw. just Justification: c(hjust, vjust) names ("left", "centre", "right", "bottom", "top") numbers [0, 1]. rot Rotation degrees, counter-clockwise.

text: sector_grob() draws batch annular sectors (pie / donut / rose wedges) single call. gp$fill recycles per sector; gp$col/lwd give uniform stroke. Passing r0 == r1 gives open arc (stroke ). Combined absolute (mm) radius "native" centre vl_arrow(), radius resolved device length render (like marker size), arc tracks mm size page size dpi; arrowhead sits tangent outer arc's end. (node-link self-loops, prefer loop_grob() — teardrop, ring.) loop_grob() draws self-loops node-link diagrams igraph-style cubic Bézier teardrop: leaves vertex (x, y) ("native" anchor), bulges size along angle, returns, optional vl_arrow() head tangent curve returning foot. size foot absolute resolved device px render, loop fixed physical size scales mm node markers — native-per-mm estimation, exact figure size/dpi.

text: unit vector.

title: Units of measurement — vl_unit
text: Supported units: "npc" — normalised parent coordinates (0 = bottom/left, 1 = top/right) "native" — enclosing viewport's xscale/yscale "mm", "cm", "", "pt" — absolute lengths "char", "line" — font-relative (need fontsize/lineheight via data) "strwidth", "strheight" — size string (need label via data) Font- string-relative units resolved absolute millimetres construction (text metrics available device-independently), stored unit ever holds one core backend units. Arithmetic: + - combine two units code, two absolute units ("mm"/"cm"/""/"pt"), resolve "mm" immediately (e.g. vl_unit(10, "mm") + vl_unit(1, "") 35.4mm). position unit ("npc"/"native") plus absolute unit forms compound unit — data/panel anchor plus exact absolute offset — e.g. vl_unit(1, "native") + vl_unit(2, "mm") 1native+2mm: resolves native position shifted right exactly 2 mm render, scale aspect. Mixing two different position bases (e.g. "npc" "native") still errors, reduced one unit. unit * scalar scales base value offset together.

NULL (default) means fully opaque. blend Optional blend mode compositing viewport's contents (isolated layer) onto backdrop . One "normal" (default), "multiply", "screen", "overlay", "darken", "lighten", "color-dodge", "color-burn", "hard-light", "soft-light", "difference", "exclusion", "hue", "saturation", "color", "luminosity" (CSS mix-blend-mode set). Flag viewport's subtree cached sub-raster: render rasterised layer , later renders subtree unchanged, cached pixels composited instead re-drawing subtree. makes partial redraw cheap — highlight/hover (edit_node() one element re-render) animation (one subtree changes, others static) re-rasterise changed. Raster/display() ; SVG/PDF ignore render subtree vector (fidelity loss). Ignored viewport also sets non-normal blend (blend needs live backdrop). See vl_clear_render_cache(). widths, heights Track sizes vl_unit() vector. Use "null" units flexible tracks share leftover space proportion value. respect Logical; TRUE, lock layout's aspect grid-style: one unit "null" width forced physical (device) size one unit "null" height. axis whose null unit larger shrinks match whole grid centered parent (absolute gutter tracks stay attached flexible cells). Encode desired cell aspect null track weights — cell null width-weight w height-weight h renders device aspect w:h. Default FALSE (tracks just fill parent). fixed-aspect panel (e.g. coord_fixed(), maps) built top vellum.

title: vellum 0.4.0
text: Faster, leaner keyed SVG emission. Emitting scene SVG per-element data-keys (interactivity attributes) now writes element straight output buffer instead building copying intermediate string per element, memoises fill attribute across batch’s shared paint, holds current element key Rc<str>, skips escaping work keys/ids/labels contain XML metacharacters (common case). Output byte-identical; 150k-point keyed scatter’s scene_svg() ~12% faster substantially fewer per-element allocations. vl_strwidth() / vl_strheight() measure md() labels. now accept rich label md() (list ) addition character strings, measuring run composition renderer draws — super/subscripts bold runs reserve space actually occupy. family/fontface/fontsize supply base style label’s runs relative . Previously caller reduce rich label plain text (.character() md() object errors), downstream layout code measured rich title got zero width clipped . Line & segment datashading. datashade_lines() datashade_segments() extend aggregate--shade engine point clouds dense lines. new anti-aliased Rust line rasteriser accumulates coverage per grid cell (overlapping lines add), bundle hundreds timeseries graph tens thousands edges renders cost decoupled vertex count, one raster_grob(). datashade_lines() takes connected polyline optional group id (packing many series one call; NA also breaks line); datashade_segments() takes independent (x0,y0)->(x1,y1) segments (network-edge case). share datashade()’s colors//span/clip shading per-line weight. See Datashading article inst/examples/lines.R. Pixel spreading (spread() / dynspread()). Dilate non-empty pixels raster grob thin marks stay visible — datashader’s spread (fixed radius) dynspread (radius chosen image density). Available standalone, via spread = argument datashade* functions (spread = 2 fixed radius, spread = "auto" dynspread). Focal / two-circle radial gradients. radial_gradient() gained fx, fy, fr — focal (start) circle stop offset 0, distinct outer (end) circle cx/cy/r offset 1. Offsetting fx/fy moves highlight -centre (sphere lit one side); non-zero fr gives annular ramp two circles. matches grid’s two-circle radialGradient() (previous concentric-form place highlight dead-centre). defaults (fx = cx, fy = cy, fr = 0) old concentric behaviour byte--byte unchanged every backend. Rendered identically raster (tiny-skia two-point conical), SVG (<radialGradient fx fy fr>), PDF (krilla). See inst/examples/gradients.R. Hue-preserving (OKLCH) gradient interpolation. linear_gradient() radial_gradient() now also accept interpolation = "oklch", polar form Oklab (lightness, chroma, hue). Hue chroma move independently, ramp two saturated colours keeps chroma middle instead desaturating toward grey way straight line Oklab can — hue sweeps along shorter arc (blue→yellow passes green). achromatic endpoint (grey/black/white) borrows end’s hue, ramps /white don’t flash arbitrary colour. Like "oklab" pre-sampled dense sRGB stops Rust core, renders identically raster, SVG, PDF backends new dependency. See inst/examples/gradient-interpolation.R side--side three spaces. Perceptual (Oklab) gradient interpolation. linear_gradient() radial_gradient() gained interpolation = "oklab", blends stops perceptually-uniform Oklab space instead sRGB — removing muddy, -dark midtones hue drift sRGB blending (blue→yellow ramp longer passes grey dead-zone). works identically raster, SVG, PDF backends: stops pre-sampled Oklab dense sRGB stops, backend colour-space support needed. default "srgb" byte--byte unchanged. (Implemented directly, new crate dependency.) Two new marker shapes: "triangle_down" "star". points_grob(shape=) now accepts downward-pointing triangle five-pointed star addition circle/square/triangle/diamond/plus/cross. Like filled shapes paint gp$fill outline gp$col (open marker fill = NA col), take solid-fill fast path. grid-device shim now maps pch 6/25 triangle_down (previously collapsed onto -triangle) pch 8 star. Slimmer Rust dependency tree. Bumped direct tiny-skia (0.11 → 0.12) skrifa (0.31 → 0.42) crates versions krilla PDF backend already pulls . collapses six duplicated transitive crates (skrifa, read-fonts, font-types, tiny-skia-path, png, bitflags compiled twice), taking vendored tree 75 69 crates smaller source tarball faster build. Rendered output unchanged — raster snapshots pixel--pixel identical — minimum Rust version still 1.92.

title: vellum 0.3.0
text: Categorical datashading (datashade(category=)). datashade() gained count_cat mode: pass category (factor vector, one value per point) category aggregated count grid single pass, every cell coloured count-weighted average category hues holds, opacity cell’s total density. shows category dominates — categories mix — without overplotting bias, one call instead hand-stacked layer per category. category set, colors per-category hue vector (named level, one per level level order) rather low--high ramp. Backed new rs_aggregate_2d_cat() Rust aggregator (one O(N) pass, category-major grid). datashade() call category renders byte--byte . Percentile / span colour clamping datashade(). New span (absolute c(lo, hi) density limits) clip (percentile pair like c(0.01, 0.99), derived non-empty cell quantiles) clamp density range transform, extreme cells longer flatten rest. default NULL (unchanged output). shade step now reusable internal colormap utility shared density categorical paths.

title: vellum 0.2.0
text: Breaking: renamed grid-colliding exports vl_ prefix attaching vellum longer masks grid: gpar() → vl_gpar(), unit() → vl_unit(), viewport() → vl_viewport(), arrow() → vl_arrow(), pattern() → vl_pattern(). old names removed (aliases). Multi-line per-datum rich text. text_grob() labels may now contain embedded newlines (\n), stacked baseline--baseline; md() gained now vectorised — md(x) returns single label length-1 x list labels vector, label grob can carry one distinct rich label per position. Single-line, single-label output byte--byte unchanged. (Rust untouched — shaping stays R-side.) Accessibility (a11y). vl_scene(title=, desc=) new describe() setter attach accessible name long description (alt text) scene. set: SVG backend marks root <svg role="img" aria-labelledby=…> emits <title>/<desc> (WCAG 1.1.1); PDF backend produces tagged PDF — chart Figure structure tree carrying description Alt text. Purely additive: scene title/desc renders byte--byte . (Strict PDF/UA-1 validation planned follow-; tag tree + Alt ship now.) datashade(weight=) now recycles scalar errors wrong-length vector, instead silently discarding mismatched weight reverting plain count.

title: vellum 0.1.1
text: Compound native + mm / npc + mm units. position unit combined absolute unit now forms compound unit — data/panel anchor plus exact absolute offset — instead erroring. unit(1, "native") + unit(2, "mm") resolves native position shifted exactly 2 mm render, scale aspect (offset applied device-side base resolves). deferred “B1” route; unlocks device-exact label nudges, halos, drop-shadow offsets grammar layer. Mixing two different position bases (e.g. npc native) still errors. Unit arithmetic scales base offset together. Additive change: scene using compound units renders byte--byte .

title: vellum 0.1.0

title: vellum 0.1.0

Output byte-stable snapshot-testable. display() draws scene active graphics device; scene_raster() / scene_svg() return rendered scene memory.
